title: pittosporum perryanum
intro: Pittosporum perryanum, also known as the 'Kauri', is a native New Zealand evergreen tree. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties, and has been used to treat various health conditions such as skin irritations, wounds, and infections. The health benefits of Pittosporum perryanum are attributed to its antioxidant and antimicrobial properties, which may help to protect against cell damage and infection. Herbal preparations of Pittosporum perryanum can be made into a tincture, salve, or infusion, and are often used topically to promote wound healing and soothe skin irritations.
